conditioning: vowel-harmony (front/back + rounding) on every suffix; consonant assimilation (d~t after voiceless; buffer y/n after vowels)

fit_none.note: plural and possessive suffixes co-occur on the same nouns but are NOT case — the expected confound the detector must separate from the 6 cases

evidence_citations.claim: noun suffixes co-vary with grammatical role
evidence_citations.stat: projected dep-role covariation: distinct suffix families concentrate on nsubj/obj/obl
case suffix families recur across >=4 distinct stems each
each case has front/back (+rounding) allomorphs grouped by consonant skeleton

prose: tur has a six-term suffixal CASE system (detected, high confidence), realised by word-final suffixes that co-vary with grammatical role and harmonise with the stem vowel. Nominative is zero-marked (subjects). Accusative -(y)I marks definite objects; dative -(y)A direction/recipient; locative -DA static location; ablative -DAn source; genitive -(n)In possessor. Every suffix has front/back (+rounding) allomorphs (vowel harmony) and d~t consonant alternation. Plural -lAr and possessive suffixes stack on the same nouns but are NOT case and must be separated out. Recommendation: PROMOTE the case inventory; the oblique cases (dat/loc/abl) are the hardest to separate from a parallel-text pivot that lumps them as 'oblique', so expect the data-driven detector to under-separate them.
